content.thinking: Let me search more broadly for mopt_* files.

I found the files. They are MP4 files with dimensions encoded in their filenames. I need to:
1. Convert them to WebM format
2. Export at 100%, 90%, 80%, 70% quality
3. Resize to the dimensions specified in the filenames

The files are:
- mopt_review-2_270x480.mp4 → 270x480
- mopt_review-1_270x480.mp4 → 270x480
- mopt_review-3_270x480.mp4 → 270x480
- mopt_hero-vial-with-text_600x600.mp4 → 600x600


- mopt_hero-vial-with-text_600x600.mp4 → 600x600

For the WebM conversion, I'll use ffmpeg with VP9 codec, where I can control quality through CRF values—lower numbers mean better quality, ranging from 0 (lossless) to 63 (worst). I'll map the quality percentages to appropriate CRF values, with 100% quality at CRF 10 and progressively higher CRF values for the lower quality tiers. I'm settling on a practical linear mapping where CRF 10 represents maximum quality and CRF 40 represents 70% quality, then I'll name the output files with the quality percentage and dimensions included.

Now I need to verify that ffmpeg is available on the system before proceeding.

content.thinking:
Good, ffmpeg is available and it has libvpx (VP9 codec support). Now let me create the conversion script.

For WebM with VP9:
- Quality is controlled by CRF (0 = lossless, 63 = worst)
- For 100% quality: CRF 0 (lossless, but large files)
- For 90%: CRF ~18
- For 80%: CRF ~28
- For 70%: CRF ~38

Actually let me use CRF values that are more practical:
- 100% → CRF 10 (near lossless, good quality)


- 90% → CRF 20
- 80% → CRF 30
- 70% → CRF 40

I'll name the output files with quality suffixes like `q100`, `q90`, `q80`, `q70`. Looking at the input filenames, I can see the dimensions are embedded in them—like `mopt_review-2_270x480.mp4` has 270x480 resolution. Since WebM/VP9 requires even dimensions and these are already even, I should be fine there.

The user wants me to resize to the dimensions specified in the filenames and convert to WebM at four quality levels, so I'll write a bash script that processes all four files and generates 16 output files total. I need to verify the actual file dimensions match what's in the names first, then scale accordingly using ffmpeg.

For the VP9 codec, I'll map the quality levels to CRF values—100% quality gets CRF 10, 90% gets CRF 20, 80% gets CRF 31, and 70% gets CRF 41. I should also check if the source files have audio and include it using the libopus codec if they do. The output files will follow the naming pattern of the original base name plus a quality suffix like `_q100.webm`.

Writing the bash script now...

content.text: Good, ffmpeg is available with VP9 support. Now I'll process all 4 files × 4 quality levels = 16 WebM outputs. Quality maps to VP9 CRF values (lower = better): 100%→CRF 10, 90%→CRF 20, 80%→CRF 31, 70%→CRF 41.

input.command:
for file in mopt_*.mp4; do
  base="${file%.mp4}"
  dims=$(echo "$base" | grep -oE '[0-9]+x[0-9]+$')
  w=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f1)
  h=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f2)
  echo "Processing: $file → ${w}x${h}"
  for q in 100 90 80 70; do
    case $q in
      100) crf=10 ;;
      90)  crf=20 ;;
      80)  crf=31 ;;
      70)  crf=41 ;;
    esac
    out="${base}_q${q}.webm"
    echo "  → $out (CRF $crf)"
    ffmpeg -y -i "$file" \
      -vf "scale=${w}:${h}" \
      -c:v libvpx-vp9 -crf $crf -b:v 0 \
      -c:a libopus -b:a 96k \
      -deadline good -cpu-used 2 \
      "$out" 2>&1 | tail -3
    echo "  Done: $out ($(du -sh "$out" | cut -f1))"
  done
done
echo "All done."
input.description: Convert all mopt_*.mp4 to WebM at 4 quality levels using VP9 + Opus

content.text:
All 16 WebM files generated successfully. Here's a summary:

| File | Dimensions | q100 | q90 | q80 | q70 |
|------|-----------|------|-----|-----|-----|
| `mopt_hero-vial-with-text` | 600×600 | 2.0M | 1.0M | 546K | 299K |
| `mopt_review-1` | 270×480 | 6.5M | 3.7M | 2.1M | 1.2M |
| `mopt_review-2` | 270×480 | 9.6M | 5.3M | 3.0M | 1.7M |
| `mopt_review-3` | 270×480 | 3.6M | 2.0M | 1.2M | 794K |

- Codec: VP9 video + Opus audio
- Dimensions: pulled from each filename (`_WxH`)
- Quality → VP9 CRF mapping: 100%→CRF 10, 90%→CRF 20, 80%→CRF 31, 70%→CRF 41

content.thinking:
The user wants to also generate WebM files at 40%, 50%, and 60% quality levels. Let me extend the CRF mapping:
- 60% → CRF ~51
- 50% → CRF ~51... 

Wait, let me think about the linear mapping again. The CRF range is 0-63 for VP9.

Using the existing mapping as reference:
- 100% → CRF 10
- 90% → CRF 20
- 80% → CRF 31
- 70% → CRF 41

So roughly each 10% step = ~10 CRF points. Continuing:


- 60% → CRF 51
- 50% → CRF 57 (maybe round to 57)
- 40% → CRF 63 (max)

I'll stick with 60% at CRF 51, 50% at CRF 57, and 40% at CRF 63 to cap out at the maximum quality setting.

input.command:
for file in mopt_*.mp4; do
  base="${file%.mp4}"
  dims=$(echo "$base" | grep -oE '[0-9]+x[0-9]+$')
  w=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f1)
  h=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f2)
  for q in 60 50 40; do
    case $q in
      60) crf=51 ;;
      50) crf=57 ;;
      40) crf=63 ;;
    esac
    out="${base}_q${q}.webm"
    ffmpeg -y -i "$file" \
      -vf "scale=${w}:${h}" \
      -c:v libvpx-vp9 -crf $crf -b:v 0 \
      -c:a libopus -b:a 96k \
      -deadline good -cpu-used 2 \
      "$out" 2>&1 | tail -1
    echo "Done: $out ($(du -sh "$out" | cut -f1))"
  done
done
echo "All done."
input.description: Convert mopt_*.mp4 to WebM at q40, q50, q60 quality levels

content.thinking: 75% quality → CRF between 70% (CRF 41) and 80% (CRF 31), so CRF 36.

input.command:
for file in /Users/jbiggs/Downloads/mopt_*.mp4; do
  base="${file%.mp4}"
  dims=$(echo "$base" | grep -oE '[0-9]+x[0-9]+$')
  w=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f1)
  h=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f2)
  out="${base}_q75.webm"
  ffmpeg -y -i "$file" \
    -vf "scale=${w}:${h}" \
    -c:v libvpx-vp9 -crf 36 -b:v 0 \
    -c:a libopus -b:a 96k \
    -deadline good -cpu-used 2 \
    "$out" 2>&1 | tail -1
  echo "Done: $(basename $out) ($(du -sh "$out" | cut -f1))"
done
input.description: Convert mopt_*.mp4 to WebM at 75% quality (CRF 36)

input.command:
for file in /Users/jbiggs/Downloads/mopt_*.mp4; do
  base="${file%.mp4}"
  dims=$(echo "$base" | grep -oE '[0-9]+x[0-9]+$')
  w=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f1)
  h=$(echo "$dims" | cut -dx -f2)
  for q in 60 50 40; do
    case $q in
      60) crf=51 ;;
      50) crf=57 ;;
      40) crf=63 ;;
    esac
    out="${base}_q${q}.webm"
    ffmpeg -y -i "$file" \
      -vf "scale=${w}:${h}" \
      -c:v libvpx-vp9 -crf $crf -b:v 0 \
      -c:a libopus -b:a 96k \
      -deadline good -cpu-used 2 \
      "$out" 2>&1 | tail -1
    echo "Done: $(basename $out) ($(du -sh "$out" | cut -f1))"
  done
done
input.description: Re-export mopt_*.mp4 to WebM at q40, q50, q60
